extraction.one_line_summary: VINGEGAARD Jonas attacked on final ramps of Plateau de Solaison (8-14% gradients), took stage while ROGLIČ secured GC without a stage win
tactical_patterns: VINGEGAARD allowed stage win despite ROGLIČ having GC locked, revealing Tour de France co-leadership tension/token gesture dynamics.
Jumbo-Visma deployed VAN AERT through 16km descent after Grand Borno, then KUSS engine work before VINGEGAARD attack.
GUERREIRO David and GOARDU David both fell from top-10 on final climb despite two-rider GROUPAMA-FDJ breakaway strategy.
KUSS drove pace on Plateau de Solaison before VINGEGAARD attacked, suggesting designated super-domestique role limits individual GC ambitions.

rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: positive: attacked on steepest final ramps (8-14%) after Kuss work, signaling stage-race finishing strength
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: neutral: won GC without stage win, allowed Vingegaard victory suggests political/morale management within team
rider_signals.O'CONNOR Ben: positive: podium stage and GC third confirms AG2R Tour leadership, stayed with Jumbo duo longer than expected
rider_signals.KUSS Steven: positive: massive engine-room effort on final climb may have secured Tour selection despite no personal result
rider_signals.CHÁVEZ Esteban: positive: strong GC finish (7th) underscores EF Tour plans for veteran climber support role
rider_signals.GOARDU David: negative: dropped from top-10 on final climb despite two-man FDJ breakaway support reveals altitude/length vulnerability
rider_signals.GUERREIRO Ruben: negative: fell from contention on final climb, concerning for stage race ambitions
